title: KIRegister Agent-Ready Distribution
summary: KIRegister exposes a controlled, read-first product and evidence surface so external agents can discover, evaluate and prepare procurement without bypassing human responsibility.
purpose: Make KIRegister understandable and usable for enterprise, procurement, legal and AI-workflow agents while preserving review-first governance, consent gates and auditability.

systems.name: KIRegister Demo and Procurement Dossier Generator
KIRegister MCP or Apps integration layer
Human approval and governance review gates

workflow.summary: External agents read public product and policy metadata, start read-only demos, generate draft procurement dossiers and hand approval-required actions to humans.

triggers: External enterprise agent evaluates AI governance register options
Procurement or legal agent requests a product fit dossier
Human operator enables agent-facing discovery or MCP access
KIRegister product team runs the Sprint 99-112 implementation block
steps: Publish public discovery metadata and action boundaries
Expose read-only demo artifacts and sample exports
Generate procurement dossiers as drafts for human review
Expose read-only MCP or Apps SDK tools where useful
Classify every agent action as read-only, approval-required or blocked
Record relevant demo and dossier activity for audit export
Keep checkout, source connections and formal decisions behind human approval
humansInLoop: Humans approve paid activation, source connections and any workspace creation
Humans remain responsible for formal risk classification and legal review
Admins control authenticated agent access from organisation settings
Product and engineering review all new agent-facing claims before release
risks: Agent-facing discovery could accidentally expose internal workspace data
Agentic commerce could be misread as consent to purchase without a human
Too many protocol surfaces could distract from the core KIRegister workflow
Marketing-style agent copy could undermine the governance product tone
controls: Public endpoints are read-only and return only public product metadata or curated demo data
AgentActionPolicy classifies critical actions before implementation
Human approval is required for checkout, source connection, submission and formal governance decisions
Dossier and demo activity is recorded for audit export without secrets
